article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: Multiple attribution rule violations: the model flagged quoted and clearly attributed claims as author bias, misapplied 'Omission of Source Attribution,' and only one headline-related item is arguably author-level. Overall, high false positives and attribution errors.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged two direct quotes from City Attorney Chiu and one attributed claim as author bias, violating the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: 3 of 4 items are not author prose: two are quotes, one is an attributed claim; only the headline item is arguably valid.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article’s prose contains little clear author-level bias beyond a potentially sensational subhead; no major omissions.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are largely moot due to attribution errors. The headline labeled as Sensationalism is debatable but defensible; other labels misapplied.
